findings.title: Silent catch in tick() has no test and swallows all errors
findings.description: The catch block in ScreenshotPipeline.tick() silently swallows all errors (capture failures, pHash failures, VLM failures) and returns false with no logging and no distinction from a dedup skip. There is no test in screenshot-pipeline.test.ts that verifies error behavior: what happens when screenshotProvider.capture() throws, when computePHash throws on corrupt data, or when vlmProvider.describeImage() throws. A caller receiving false cannot distinguish 'deduped' from 'crashed'. This violates the error-handling rules: catch without logging or re-throwing typed error. Additionally, the comment says 'this would use a structured logger' which is a TODO in disguise.
findings.suggestion: 1. Add tests for each failure mode: (a) screenshotProvider.capture() rejects, (b) corrupt image causes computePHash to fail, (c) vlmProvider.describeImage() rejects. Verify tick() returns false (or a typed result that distinguishes dedup from error). 2. Replace the bare catch with at minimum a structured logger call. 3. Consider returning a discriminated union like { status: 'processed' | 'deduped' | 'error', error?: Error } instead of a boolean so callers can observe failures.

findings.title: computePHash not tested with invalid/corrupt image buffer
findings.description: The phash.test.ts tests cover valid images of various sizes and colors, but there is no test for invalid input: an empty buffer (Buffer.alloc(0)), a non-image buffer (Buffer.from('not-an-image')), or a truncated/corrupt PNG. Since computePHash calls sharp which will throw on invalid input, the test suite should document and verify this error behavior. The function has no try/catch, so sharp errors propagate uncaught -- this is fine if intentional, but it should be tested to lock in the contract.
findings.suggestion: Add boundary tests: (1) computePHash(Buffer.alloc(0)) should reject/throw with a meaningful error. (2) computePHash(Buffer.from('not-an-image')) should reject/throw. These tests document the error contract for callers (like ScreenshotPipeline.tick() which catches all errors).

findings.title: hammingDistance not tested with empty strings
findings.description: The hammingDistance tests cover identical, single-bit-different, completely opposite, and length-mismatch cases. However, the empty string boundary is not tested: hammingDistance('', '') should return 0 (both empty, no differing bits). While this is a minor edge case since the function is always called with 64-char hashes in practice, testing empty inputs documents the contract. Also, the error test on line 127 does not verify the error message content -- it only checks that it throws.
findings.suggestion: Add: (1) expect(hammingDistance('', '')).toBe(0) to document the empty-string contract. (2) Update the throw test to verify the error message: expect(() => hammingDistance('01', '011')).toThrow('Hash length mismatch: 2 vs 3').

findings.title: ScreenshotProcessor error wrapping test does not verify cause chain
findings.description: The error wrapping test verifies the outer error message but does not verify the error cause chain. The ScreenshotProcessor uses `new Error('Screenshot processing failed', { cause: error })` which preserves the original error. The test should verify that error.cause contains the original 'API rate limit exceeded' error. Without this assertion, a regression that drops the cause (e.g., replacing with a plain `throw new Error('Screenshot processing failed')`) would not be caught. Compare with screenshot.test.ts which correctly uses `.rejects.toSatisfy()` to verify the cause chain.

positive_observations: phash.ts tests are exemplary Functional Core unit tests: real sharp library for image creation, pure input/output verification, no mocks whatsoever

All Test Doubles for external boundaries (ScreenshotProvider, VlmProvider) include explicit '// Test Double rationale:' comments documenting why a real dependency is not used -- exactly matching the project convention
ScreenshotPipeline tests exercise the dedup logic thoroughly via direct tick() calls rather than relying on timers, making tests deterministic and fast
areSimilar() tests cover both custom thresholds and the default threshold, documenting the API contract clearly

screenshot-pipeline.test.ts uses real PNG images with different spatial patterns (horizontal vs vertical split) to produce genuinely different pHashes, avoiding the solid-color pitfall where all hashes would be identical
